title: Store: cursor + verified_at on brain_profile_cache (migration + accessors)
userStory: As the resolver, I want the brain's opaque change cursor and a verified_at stamp persisted alongside the cached brief, so I can tell 'confirmed current as of T' apart from 'written at T' and skip work when nothing moved.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration internal/store/migrations/0037_brain_profile_cursor.sql adds `cursor TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''` and `verified_at TEXT` to brain_profile_cache (nullable verified_at so a pre-existing row reads as never-verified)

store.BrainProfile (internal/store/brain_profile.go) gains Cursor and VerifiedAt fields, plus a VerifiedAgeSeconds int64 computed in SQL the same way AgeSeconds is (now - verified_at; left as a sentinel like -1 when verified_at IS NULL so callers don't parse timestamps)
GetBrainProfile selects the two new columns + verified_age_seconds
PutBrainProfile (the full write) gains a cursor parameter and stamps verified_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP alongside fetched_at — a fresh distill is by definition verified-now

New TouchBrainProfile(sourceURL, cursor string) error updates cursor + verified_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ONLY, leaving body / content_hash / fetched_at untouched — the 'confirmed unchanged' stamp used by the Tier 0/Tier 1 no-op paths. Returns sql.ErrNoRows-safe (no-op if the row is absent is acceptable; document the choice)
All existing PutBrainProfile callers updated to pass a cursor (resolver passes the cursor from /changes; pre-existing callers that have none pass "")

[Verification] go build ./... passes; a throwaway test or a `scout serve --addr :48xx --db /tmp/cp-US001.db` boot proves the migration applies cleanly on a fresh DB and on a DB created before 0037 (column added, old row's verified_at NULL). Insert via PutBrainProfile, TouchBrainProfile with a new cursor, then GetBrainProfile and confirm body/content_hash unchanged but cursor + verified_at moved.
